Health.headline: 'Every health facility said they were full': alarm over rapid spread of Ebola in DRC
Health.detail: Health workers and aid groups are expressing deep concern over the rapid spread of the Ebola virus in the Democratic Republic of Congo, where the fragile healthcare system is struggling to cope with the outbreak and international support is urgently needed. Nearly 750 suspected cases and 177 suspected deaths have been recorded since the first known victim died on April 24.

Health.headline: Attack On Ebola Hospital In Eastern Congo Echoes Past Violence Against Health Workers
Health.detail: An angry crowd set fire to Ebola isolation tents outside a hospital in the Democratic Republic of Congo's (DRC) Ituri province after the family of a young man who died of the virus was refused permission to take his body to be buried. The World Health Organization (WHO) has raised its risk assessment of the DRC outbreak from “high” to “very high” for the country, citing the rapid spread of cases and growing insecurity.

The World Health Organization has declared the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo, caused by the rare Bundibugyo strain, a global health emergency. The outbreak has already killed dozens.
Health.headline: WHO declares Ebola outbreak in DRC a global health emergency

Health.detail: Scientists have identified potentially cancer-causing chemicals, known as PAHs, hiding in many everyday foods, especially those exposed to high heat cooking methods like grilling, roasting, smoking, and frying. The compounds can form during cooking.
Health.headline: Scientists Uncover Cancer-causing Chemicals Hidden in Everyday Foods

Health.detail: A recent study has found a correlation between higher caffeine intake and greater severity of depression. The study was presented at APA 2026.
Health.headline: Higher Caffeine Intake Tied to Greater Depression Severity

Lifestyle.detail: Aishwarya Rai Bachchan appeared at Cannes 2026 in a stunning powder pink gown with a cape, making a grand statement.
Lifestyle.headline: Cannes 2026: Aishwarya Rai Bachchan is a sight to behold in powder pink gown with cape
Experts call moong dal the traditional Indian superfood: 'Desi ghee daalo isme'
Lifestyle.detail: Nutrition experts are highlighting moong dal as a traditional Indian superfood, emphasizing its health benefits when prepared with desi ghee.
Airbnb is broadening its services to include boutique hotels, car rentals, and grocery delivery to become a comprehensive travel platform.
Lifestyle.headline: Airbnb expands into hotels, cars, groceries

Lifestyle.detail: Singaporean artist Amanda Heng's 'A Pause' installation at the Venice Biennale encourages visitors to slow down and experience stillness, reflecting on the idea of rest.
Lifestyle.headline: 'Find a moment for yourself': Why Singaporean artist Amanda Heng is inviting people to just rest in Venice
Meera Sodha's vegetarian recipe for crispy one-pan spaghetti with gochujang and mozzarella
Lifestyle.detail: Meera Sodha shares a vegetarian recipe for a crispy one-pan spaghetti dish featuring gochujang and mozzarella.

Entertainment.headline: Former Prince Andrew faces widening misconduct probe as police launch witness hunt
Entertainment.detail: The investigation into Prince Andrew's alleged misconduct is expanding as police seek additional witnesses.

Entertainment.headline: Netflix's Virgin River star found dead as police launch murder investigation
Entertainment.detail: A star from the Netflix series 'Virgin River' was found dead, and police have opened a murder investigation.
Irish rock band The Script has announced a new album and a large-scale tour across the UK and Ireland.
Entertainment.headline: The Script announce new album 'The User's Guide To Being Human' and massive UK & Ireland arena tour
Britney Spears argument, DUI arrest on video
Entertainment.detail: Newly released video shows Britney Spears' DUI arrest in March 2026, where officers smelled alcohol and conducted a sobriety test.
A Ricky Martin concert was temporarily halted due to a tear gas incident affecting the audience.
Entertainment.headline: Ricky Martin Concert Interrupted by Tear Gas Incident

Crime & Justice.headline: Regions Bank to Pay $4.9 Million to Resolve Civil Liability in Connection with Ineligible Paycheck Protection Program Loan
Crime & Justice.detail: Regions Bank will pay $4.9 million to resolve civil liability related to an ineligible Paycheck Protection Program loan. The resolution demonstrates the Justice Department's commitment to ensuring the integrity of federal programs.

Crime & Justice.headline: 12 Members of Violent “Crown Hill Enterprise” in Indianapolis Indicted on Federal RICO Charges, Including Murder
Crime & Justice.detail: Twelve members of the violent “Crown Hill Enterprise” in Indianapolis have been indicted on federal RICO charges, including murder. This indictment is part of an effort to combat violent crime in the city.

Members of a transnational money laundering organization have been charged with laundering cartel funds. The charges are the result of a multi-agency investigation targeting international criminal networks.
Crime & Justice.headline: Members of Transnational Money Laundering Organization Charged with Laundering Cartel Funds

Crime & Justice.detail: The Fraud Division announced the expansion of its Midwest Task Force and authorization to hire 15 new Medicaid prosecutors, highlighted an unprecedented Minnesota health care fraud takedown, and revealed a $2 Billion Telemedicine Health Care Fraud Scheme. These actions underscore the Justice Department's commitment to combating fraud and protecting taxpayer dollars.
Crime & Justice.headline: This Week in Fraud: The Fraud Division Announced Expansion of Midwest Task Force and Authorization to Hire 15 New Medicaid Prosecutors, an Unprecedented Minnesota Health Care Fraud Takedown, and a $2 Billion Telemedicine Health Care Fraud Scheme
Feeding Our Future Ringleader Sentenced to 500 Months
Crime & Justice.detail: A ringleader in the Feeding Our Future fraud case has been sentenced to 500 months in prison. This sentence reflects the severity of the crime and the Justice Department's commitment to prosecuting those who exploit federal programs.

Technology.headline: OpenAI Autonomously Disproves the Erdős Unit Distance Conjecture — 80-Year-Old Math Problem Solved
Technology.detail: One of OpenAI's internal general-purpose reasoning models autonomously disproved a central conjecture in discrete geometry that Paul Erdős posed in 1946, establishing an infinite family of planar point configurations that produce significantly more unit-distance pairs than previously thought. The proof method approached the problem through algebraic number theory, connecting an elementary geometry question to deep number-theoretic structures.

Technology.headline: Meta's Zuckerberg Caught Training AI on Employees Before Firing 8,000 of Them
Technology.detail: A leaked audio recording revealed that Meta was tracking employee activity across Gmail, Google Chat, the internal assistant Metamate, and VS Code to train Meta's AI models, coinciding with layoff notices for approximately 8,000 employees. CEO Mark Zuckerberg defended the "Model Capability Initiative" (MCI), stating that it helps train AI models on how smart people work.

Technology.headline: AMD to invest US$10 billion in Taiwanese microchip ecosystem
Technology.detail: Advanced Micro Devices Inc (AMD) is investing US$10 billion in the Taiwanese microchip ecosystem to ensure the availability of advanced packaging technology capacity over the next three years, given rapidly growing artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure demand. The investment aims to foster a new advanced packaging ecosystem in Taiwan using elevated fan-out bridge (EFB) technology.
SpaceX is set to launch Starship V3 for its first test flight today. The launch aims to test the newest and biggest version of Starship.
Technology.headline: SpaceX will launch Starship V3 for its first test flight today

Technology.detail: TECSUN SCIENCE & TECHNOLOGY CO.,LTD. and QingCloud Technologies Corp. are partnering to explore a new paradigm of livelihood services featuring “AI + Cloud + Computing Power Collaboration”, focusing on core sectors including employment, healthcare and government services. The collaboration aims to promote the integrated deployment of employment Agent and intelligent computing platform to achieve precise job matching and intelligent recommendation.
Technology.headline: TECSUN SCIENCE & TECHNOLOGY CO.,LTD. Partners with QingCloud Technologies Corp. to Forge New Engine for AI-Powered Livelihood Services

Education.headline: Government finds school program violated law in wake of deadly Okinawa boat accident
Education.detail: The education ministry reported that a high school program violated the basic education law due to political neutrality issues, following a fatal accident in Okinawa Prefecture in March involving a study trip about the relocation of a U.S. Marine Corps Air Station. The ministry criticized the school's safety management as 'extremely inappropriate,' noting the lack of preliminary inspections and supervising teachers.

The Star Education Fair 2026 in Penang features 96 exhibitors and 107 booths, offering personalized academic counseling and scholarship opportunities. The fair includes seven Cluster Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) programs spanning 56 booths from nine ministries, covering various industries.
Education.headline: Explore tertiary pathways at Star Education Fair 2026 in Penang, 96 exhibitors taking part
What the India-New Zealand FTA means for Indian students
Education.detail: An FTA between India and New Zealand may affect Indian Students. Further details are available in the article.

Education.headline: Bihar government announces free bus travel for students appearing for NEET re-examination
Education.detail: The Bihar government is offering free bus travel for students taking the NEET re-examination. More information can be found in the article.

The Computer Science Teachers Association is launching the "AI PD Weeks" initiative to provide K-12 educators with hands-on learning, collaboration, and practical strategies for teaching AI in K-12 classrooms. The $11M effort aims to train teachers in AI with hands on learning over the summer.
Education.headline: New $11M Effort Aims to Train Teachers in AI. How Does It Work?

Science.headline: NASA Stunned as Strange Solar Radio Burst Lasts 19 Days
Science.detail: NASA scientists were surprised by a solar radio signal that persisted for 19 days, far exceeding the typical duration of such bursts, and they traced the signal to a large magnetic structure on the Sun.

Science.headline: SpaceX's Starship flight hits most targets in pre-IPO test
Science.detail: SpaceX conducted a largely successful test flight of its Starship rocket, deploying mock satellites and performing a controlled splashdown in the Indian Ocean, marking a significant step as the company prepares for its IPO.

Science.headline: Scientists create teabags that remove arsenic from drinking water
Science.detail: Researchers at the City University of New York's ASRC Nanoscience Initiative have developed a 7-cent teabag that can remove 90% of arsenic ions from contaminated water, offering an affordable solution to a global health crisis.

Science.headline: Ancient woodworking technique could save modern electronics from overheating
Science.detail: Researchers found that using carpentry-inspired joints in electronic interfaces can significantly lower temperatures in chips, potentially enhancing the energy efficiency and durability of various technologies.
According to ScienceAlert, nearly half of preventable cancers are connected to just two lifestyle habits.
Science.headline: Almost 50% of Preventable Cancers Linked to Just Two Lifestyle Habits

Zimbabwe.detail: Eight Roman Catholic parishioners were killed in a commuter omnibus crash.
Two individuals were apprehended for vandalizing a ZESA transformer and possessing copper windings illegally.
Zimbabwe.headline: Pair arrested for vandalising ZESA transformer, unlawful possession of copper windings
Man busted while attempting to smuggle drugs and substances worth US$12,000 across border
Zimbabwe.detail: A man was caught trying to smuggle drugs and other substances valued at US$12,000 across the border.

Zimbabwe.headline: 'Public officials demanding bribes from investors are enemies of development': Mnangagwa
Zimbabwe.detail: President Mnangagwa stated that public officials who solicit bribes from investors are detrimental to the nation's progress.
Diaspora Insurance hosted a dinner in Birmingham to welcome the Warriors ahead of the Unity Cup.

Finance & Economy.headline: BSP mulls over off-cycle rate hike
Bank capital buffer rules tightened
Finance & Economy.detail: The regulatory body has announced the tightening of bank capital buffer rules to ensure financial stability.
SM Investments Corporation (SMIC) announced that its clean energy sourcing has increased to 31%.

SM Prime brings Ticketmaster to the Philippines
Finance & Economy.detail: SM Prime has partnered with Ticketmaster to bring its services to the Philippines.
Moody's has revised South Africa's outlook, signaling a positive move for the country's economy.
Finance & Economy.headline: Moody's 'upgrades' South Africa's outlook

Politics.headline: Three months in, is Trump losing the Iran war?
Politics.detail: Despite tactical successes against Iran, doubts are growing about whether President Trump can achieve a geopolitical win, as Iran maintains its grip on the Strait of Hormuz and resists nuclear concessions. The continuing standoff puts domestic pressure on Trump due to high gas prices and low approval ratings.

Amid ongoing negotiations, the US is reportedly considering fresh attacks on Iran. Iran's enriched uranium stockpile is a point of contention, with its supreme leader issuing a directive against sending it abroad.
Politics.headline: US weighs fresh attacks on Iran amid ongoing negotiations: Report

Politics.detail: New Zealand plans to invest approximately $936 million (USD) in drones, ship maintenance, and naval upgrades to strengthen maritime security amid concerns about supply routes. The investment includes drones for the southwest Pacific and polar-capable vehicles for the Southern Ocean.
Politics.headline: New Zealand to invest in drones and fleet to shield maritime routes
Trump's About-Face on U.S. Troops in Poland Mystifies Allies
Politics.detail: The United States is increasing its military presence in Poland by sending an additional 5,000 troops. This decision comes amidst ongoing discussions within NATO regarding regional security and defense spending, deepening European uncertainty.

Politics.headline: Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon kill at least 14 as the UN warns of rising settler violence in the occupied territories
Politics.detail: Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon resulted in at least 14 fatalities, while the UN cautioned about escalating settler violence in occupied territories. The ongoing conflict between Israel and Hezbollah is anticipated to significantly impact Lebanon's economy.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Waiting for the storm: On weather events, India's vulnerability.

World.detail: The World Health Organization warns of a rapid spread of Ebola in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, with reports indicating that health facilities are overwhelmed.
